head.hi: The President to the Secretary of State
head.note: Sent to the White House Map Room, via military channels, and forwarded by the Map Room to Hull.

div.p: In reference your message transmitted as White 67,Telegram of November 30, 1943, ante, p. 625. in view of the fact that the Russians have appointed the Russian AmbassadorFedor Tarasovich Gusev, Soviet Ambassador at London. as Soviet Representative on the Advisory Council (Commission] in London, I suggest that you announce Winant’s appointment.This was done in a press release dated December 4, published in the Department of State Bulletin, vol. ix, December 4, 1943, p. 393.

My conferences with the GeneralissimoChiang Kai-shek. were very satisfactory and I liked him. He is delighted with the results of the Moscow Conference.At this point a notation on the source text indicates a garble in the code groups.

In Teheran things went on the whole very well and better than I expected. Marshal StalinAt this point a notation on the source text indicates a garble in the code groups. and I worked together toward objectives which turned out to be very similar. I will bring you the minutes of all that was said and done. Molotov sent you his very warm personal regards.
Churchill and I are to see President Inonu here in Cairo and then I will head westward.
